2026-06-05.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability to offline and remove multiple contiguous memory ranges as one operation, ensuring atomicity and preventing race conditions that could lead to inconsistent system states. The new function, offline_and_remove_memory_ranges(), takes an array of ranges and processes them under a single lock, allowing for all-or-nothing semantics in case of failures or interruptions. This addresses issues with current implementations that require calling offline_and_remove_memory() in a loop, which can give up atomicity and lead to system inconsistencies.
